conditions.name: tier prices equal congestion externality differences
conditions.evidence: H14 observes tier shares but not value, priority, or marginal delay tolls
conditions.blocking_data: tier-specific service order, willingness-to-pay, LRD delay externality

conditions.name: router objective aligns with aggregate user surplus
conditions.evidence: public elasticity is behavioral; only H50 can identify policy effects
conditions.blocking_data: randomized router assignments plus registered user-value outcome

conditions.name: no missing money for capacity
conditions.evidence: public capacity slack is consistent with adequacy, but H48 has no capacity commitments or cost curves
conditions.blocking_data: capacity cost curves, scarcity rents, commitment delivery outcomes

conditions.name: rationing is value ordered
conditions.evidence: tier-level and toxicity/rejection patterns are descriptive, not within-tier order
conditions.blocking_data: request value/tier and admission order during shared shortages

conditions.name: no pivotal provider
conditions.evidence: multi-provider public quote competition is a proxy, not shortage-state pivotality
conditions.blocking_data: realized eligibility sets and counterfactual shortfall without each provider

conditions.name: entry fixed-cost wedge is small
conditions.evidence: entry scales weakly with token demand, consistent with cheap listing
conditions.blocking_data: provider fixed and capacity costs plus causal entry shocks

conditions.name: retry externality is internalized
conditions.evidence: market contracts do not price retries; telemetry does not yet quantify amplification
conditions.blocking_data: attempt chains, retry policy, shortage state, and user-level arrival grouping

conditions.name: endpoint fidelity is monitored
conditions.evidence: H11 cheap-bad association p=0.0872; H54 audit status not_run
conditions.blocking_data: randomized fidelity audits linked to realized selections

conditions.name: no kickback steering and harnesses are wary
conditions.evidence: price parity cannot reveal provider-router compensation or hidden steering
conditions.blocking_data: rebates/commissions or a valid randomized steering instrument

conditions.name: no common pricing delegation
conditions.evidence: BM cadence and reaction commonality do not reveal vendor identity
conditions.blocking_data: pricing-stack adoption dates or vendor-overlap disclosure

claim_boundary: A condition can be inconsistent with current evidence without proving the entire conjecture false. Unidentified conditions must never be silently treated as satisfied.
